mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests across three distinct business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in housing starts and consumer credit expansion indicates robust aggregate demand momentum, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly outperform the baseline when housing and credit growth diverge.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production peaks while consumer price inflation remains sticky above 3.0%, supply constraints combined with persistent cost pressures trigger profit margin compression, reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when industrial output stalls alongside elevated inflation, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 3.5% demonstrates accelerating economic momentum that expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat in > 50% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the threshold, or the signal fails to predict returns in a walk-forward test spanning multiple decades.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth coinciding with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms labor market resilience, boosting equity risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative or zero information coefficient over any 2-year rolling window, or equity returns do not significantly differ from baseline when payrolls and unemployment move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in consumer credit outstanding reflect expanding household balance sheets and rising consumption propensity, which historically precede positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances where consumer credit rises consecutively for three months, or the signal shows no predictive power in a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During the initial phase of a Federal Reserve tightening cycle, rising policy rates combined with elevated headline inflation compress equity valuation multiples, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when the Fed raises rates alongside sticky inflation, or the interaction term fails to predict short-term drawdowns in a walk-forward validation across multiple tightening episodes.
